client_input.title: Divorce Alimony Case
client_input.description: Wife seeking maintenance and alimony under Hindu Marriage Act

Appellant Vs. B.G.Giri ... Respondent Prayer: Civil Miscellaneous Appeal filed under Section 19 of the Hindu Marriage Act, 1955, against the fa...
citations_found.ai_summary:
Here is a 2-3 sentence summary of the Indian legal case:

The dispute in this case was about a divorce petition filed by the husband, B.G.Giri, under the Hindu Marriage Act, 1955, citing grounds of cruelty and desertion by his wife, Indra. The court, in a judgment dated December 1, 2015, upheld the divorce order and also granted permanent custody of their child to the husband due to the child's learning disability, which the court attributed to the wife's neglect. The court's decision was based on the evidence presented by the husband and his witnesses, which showed the wife's cruel and inhuman behavior towards him and their child.

citations_found.ai_summary:
Here is a 2-3 sentence summary of the case:

The dispute was about the adjustment of interim alimony granted to the wife under the Hindu Marriage Act and the Criminal Procedure Code, with the husband challenging the order of the Family Court rejecting his application for adjustment. The court ultimately decided that the Family Court's order rejecting the husband's application for adjustment of interim alimony was not sustainable and directed the Family Court to consider the husband's application for adjustment.

citations_found.ai_summary:
Here's a 2-3 sentence summary of the case:

The dispute in Disha Kushwaha vs Rituraj Singh was about a divorce and related matrimonial issues, with the wife (Disha) seeking to live with her husband (Rituraj) and the husband alleging cruelty by the wife. The court ultimately granted a decree of divorce to the husband, dismissing the wife's claims for restitution of conjugal rights and maintenance. The court's decision was based on the husband's allegations of cruelty by the wife, which the court found to be substantiated.

citations_found.ai_summary:
Here's a 2-3 sentence summary of the case:

The dispute in Sunil Bansal vs Meeta Bansal was about whether the court could extend the time for the wife to file a written statement in a divorce suit and whether the suit should be stayed until an application for alimony pendente lite was disposed of. The Calcutta High Court decided that the court cannot extend the time to file a written statement beyond the period provided under Order VIII Rule 1 of the Code of Civil Procedure, and also rejected the wife's application to stay the suit until her alimony application was disposed of.

citations_found.ai_summary:
Here's a 2-3 sentence summary of the Indian legal case:

The dispute was about a man, Gyanendra Asthana, who was ordered by a lower court to pay maintenance to his ex-wife under Section 125 of the Criminal Procedure Code, despite a previous divorce decree and a compromise agreement where he had paid her a lump sum and waived her right to claim maintenance. The Allahabad High Court, in a judgment delivered on July 21, 2023, decided to set aside the lower court's order, ruling that the maintenance proceedings were not maintainable given the previous divorce decree and compromise agreement. The court found the lower court's order to be "illegal, improper, and unfounded."
